growth_records.medium: mineral medium with acetate (electron donor) and N2O (electron acceptor); full composition in Conthe et al. 2018b/c

up.method: balance from acid dosing/off-gas at steady state

growth_records.cite: Conthe et al. O2 versus N2O respiration in a continuous microbial enrichment. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2018). DOI:10.1007/s00253-018-9247-3. [PMC6153640]
growth_records.snippet: steady-state growth on acetate oxidation coupled to N2O reduction to N2... Steady state qN2O-N -0.033±0.001, qN2-N 0.034±0.001, qAcetate-C -0.017±0.001 mmol/mmolX h-1; YXS 0.36±0.03 CmolX/CmolAc-
growth_records.notes: Chemostat D=0.026±0.001 h-1; at steady state µ=D=0.026 h-1 (calculated), td=ln2/0.026=26.6 h. Rates from Table 1 (mmol per mmol biomass-C per h): qAcetate=-0.017±0.001, qN2O-N=-0.033±0.001, qN2-N=+0.034±0.001. Table 2 Yxs(N2O)=0.36±0.03 CmolX/CmolAc, Yxe=0.16±0.01 CmolX/mol e-. 20°C, pH 7. Biomass formula CH1.8O0.5N0.2.

growth_records.medium: mineral medium with acetate (electron donor) and N2O (electron acceptor); full composition in Conthe et al. 2018b/c
growth_records.cite: Conthe et al. O2 versus N2O respiration in a continuous microbial enrichment. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2018). DOI:10.1007/s00253-018-9247-3. [PMC6153640]
growth_records.snippet: N2O batch: qN2O-N -0.131±0.004, qN2-N 0.126±0.008 mmol/mmolX h-1, qAcetate-C -0.067±0.009. The qN2Omax values identified were roughly fourfold higher than the actual rates during steady state
growth_records.notes: Table 1 N2O batch (max, non-limiting N2O), mmol/mmolX/h: qN2O-N=-0.131±0.004, qN2-N=+0.126±0.008, qAcetate-C=-0.067±0.009. No growth rate reported for batch (max-rate assay). 20°C, pH 7.

growth_records.medium: mineral medium with acetate (electron donor) and N2O (electron acceptor); full composition in Conthe et al. 2018b/c
growth_records.cite: Conthe et al. O2 versus N2O respiration in a continuous microbial enrichment. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2018). DOI:10.1007/s00253-018-9247-3. [PMC6153640]
growth_records.snippet: NO3- batch: qNO3-N -0.007±0.000, qN2-N 0.004±0.000, qAcetate-C -0.003±0.000. NO3- and NO2- reducing capacities were much lower compared to N2O or O2 (<15% of the maximum N2O or O2 reduction rate)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 NO3- batch, mmol/mmolX/h: qNO3-N=-0.007, qN2-N=+0.004, qAcetate-C=-0.003. Table 2 Yxs(NO3-)=0.38, Yxe=0.15 (steady-state ref, Conthe unpublished). 20°C, pH 7.

growth_records.snippet: N2O + NO2- batch: qN2O-N -0.033±0.000, qN2-N 0.042±0.000 mmol/mmolX h-1 (N2O gas supply kept on during addition of 1 mM KNO2-)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 N2O+NO2- batch, mmol/mmolX/h: qN2O-N=-0.033, qN2-N=+0.042. 20°C, pH 7.

growth_records.medium: mineral medium with acetate (electron donor) and N2O (electron acceptor); full composition in Conthe et al. 2018b/c

up.method: qO2max from Monod fit vs DO (Fig 2); numeric value graphical

mfa.note: ~2x lower than qN2Omax per mol acceptor, but qe- comparable

growth_records.cite: Conthe et al. O2 versus N2O respiration in a continuous microbial enrichment. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2018). DOI:10.1007/s00253-018-9247-3. [PMC6153640]
growth_records.snippet: The maximum O2 reducing capacity (qO2max) was comparable to N2O respiration when expressed per mole electron accepted... biomass yields... O2 0.45 CmolX/CmolAc-
growth_records.notes: O2-only in-situ batch (day 132). Table 2 Yxs(O2)=0.45 CmolX/CmolAc, Yxe=0.19 CmolX/mol e-. qO2max ~2x lower than qN2Omax per mol acceptor but qe- comparable (double e- per O2). Numeric qO2max only in Fig 2. 20°C, pH 7.
